ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Closing The Estate
Off Calendar Apr-28-2014 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Closing The Estate
Off Calendar Mar-24-2014 Continued To Apr-28-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrey Kalinin for Cai Zhang.

Counsel stated that he was not hired to probate the will, and that he came in at the tail end to assist with a dispute in mediation and as a favor to the disbarred attorney. Mr. Kalinin also stated that he was not paid to do anything else and that the family members were to handle the estate. The Court stated that they could petition under Probate Code 12251 for lack of assets, that if the house is in the Trust they could move forward on 12251 and that if the house is not in the trust they will have to inventory the house. Status on closing of the estate was continued to April 28, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of an inventory, a petition for final distribution or a 12251 petition.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Leonardini. (Not Reported)

Notice Of Hearing Demurrer And Demurrer To Contest Of Executors Petition For Appointment And For Letters Testamentary And To Contestants Petition For Letters Of Administration With Will Annexed; Points And Authorities; Request For Judicial Notice With Exhibits; Proof Of Service
Off Calendar Jul-14-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Canatella for and with Cai Xia Zhang (Petitioner/Executor). John Flegel for Vicky Hoang (Respondent). Kate Kostrzewa for Summco Choi (Respondent).

Mr. Canatella and Mr. Flegel confirmed that any objection based on venue is withdrawn. Counsel presented oral argument regarding who has priority for appointment as Personal Representative, standing and whether or not a demurrer is appropriate. The demurrer was taken under submission. The two probate petitions and the quiet title action on calendar for July 27, 2011 were continued to August 2, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Canatella and Mr. Flegel are to decide on an independent Person to be appointed as the Successor Personal Representative. The Court will send the Parties to a settlement conference July 27th, July 28th or July 29th.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Notice Of Hearing Demurrer And Demurrer To Contest Of Executors Petition For Appointment And For Letters Testamentary And To Contestants Petition For Letters Of Administration With Will Annexed; Points And Authorities; Request For Judicial Notice With Exhibits; Proof Of Service
Off Calendar Jun-15-2011 Continued To Jul-13-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Canatella for Cai Xia Zhang (Petitioner). John Flegel for and with Vicky Hoang (Respondent).

Counsel agreed to a settlement conference. The Court will send notice as to the time and location of the settlement conference. The issue of venue was set for July 8, 2011 at 9:00 a.m, in Dept. 204 for a half day evidentiary hearing. The Demurrer was continued to July 13, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The two competing probate petitions and the petition to determine entitlement on calendar for June 29, 2011 will be continued to a future date. Counsel is to contact the Probate Examiner by June 22, 2011 with the continued date.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

Verified Petition To Determine Title To Real And Personal Property; To Reform Documents Of Title To Real And Personal Property; To Recover Assets Of The Estate And Trust In Which Others Claim An Interest And To Assert Claims Of The Estate And Trust Against Third Parties; Exhibits
Off Calendar Jun-01-2011 Continued To Jun-29-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Canatella and David Olick for Cai Xia Zheng. John Flegel for Vicky Hoang. Gregory Rubens for Summoco Choi.

The Court stated that it had received the verified supplement to the petition, that there are some procedural issues to talk about, questioned if the petition is appropriately in San Francisco or in San Mateo, and stated that there is no special administrator acting to file the petition. Mr. Canatella stated that this is the probate of a pour-over will, that the pour-over will looks to the Trust because everything is Trust property, that they filed a petition to determine title on behalf of the Trustee, and that they used the same court number for convenience. Mr. Flegel agreed with the Court, stated that his client has an interest in the Trust property, that they will bring their own 850 petition, and that they need discovery. Mr. Rubens stated that they believe that the 850 petition is properly before the Court, that they were hoping that the Court could rule on the 850, and that they believe that the jurisdictional issues of the Trust are different issues then the Probate. Additional discussions were held regarding the procedural issues. At the conclusion of discussions, the Court continued this matter to June 29, 2011, and stated that the Demurrer currently set for June 15, 2011 would remain on calendar for that date.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.
